explanation: After 10 moves, the model increased White's win probability to 0.942 (relative to the before-game estimate of 0.880). This shift was influenced by clock consumption (with White using less time by 13.0 seconds). The Elo predictions (White: 1161 vs. actual: 1269; Black: 995 vs. actual: 792) are heavily anchor-prioritized on player causal history: White had 0 prior games and Black had 6 prior games. The actual outcome of the game was a White win.
